mnemonics: FRIP: First-in, First-out (FIFO) is the Rule In Principle. All other queues are just variations.
PRIORITY PUSH: PRIORITY queues PUSH the most important element to the front.
D-D-D: Double Ended queues have Data going in Both Directions.
keyPoints: A standard queue is FIFO (First-In-First-Out), like a line at a store.
A circular queue uses a fixed-size array and connects the end back to the start to optimize memory usage.
A priority queue dequeues elements based on priority, not arrival order (like a hospital ER).
A double-ended queue (deque) allows insertion and deletion from both the front and the rear.
commonMistakes: Assuming a 'full' circular queue has all indices occupied (it uses one empty slot to distinguish between full and empty).
Forgetting to re-heapify a priority queue after an operation, breaking its order.
Implementing a standard queue with an inefficient data structure (e.g., a list) causing O(n) dequeue operations instead of O(1).
